children.text: Once upon a time, there was a mother duck. She had six little ducklings.
children.cn: 从前，有一只鸭妈妈。她有六只小鸭子。
children.notes: Once upon a time 从前。
children.statement: was 是 be 动词 is 的过去式。
children.explanation: 叙述故事时通常使用过去时态。

children.text: Five of the ducklings were small and yellow, but the last duckling was big and grey.
children.cn: 其中五只小鸭子又小又黄，但最后一只小鸭子又大又灰。
children.statement: grey 也可以拼写为 gray。
children.explanation: grey 是英式拼法，gray 是美式拼法。
children.keywords: Five small yellow, last big grey

children.text: 'He doesn't look like us! What an ugly duckling!' the other ducklings said.
children.cn: '他看起来不像我们！多么丑的一只小鸭子啊！'其他的小鸭子说。
children.statement: look like 在句中表示“看起来像”。
children.explanation: look 是连系动词，like 是介词。
children.keywords: doesn't look like us, ugly duckling

children.text: 'That's not nice!' said Mother Duck. 'He's still a duck like us!'
children.cn: '那不太好！'鸭妈妈说。'他仍然是像我们一样的鸭子！'
children.statement: duck 在这里是动词，意为“躲避”。
children.explanation: 在这里 duck 是名词，表示“鸭子”。

children.text: At the river, the ugly duckling swam well, just like all the other ducklings.
children.cn: 在河边，丑小鸭游得很好，就像所有其他的小鸭子一样。
children.notes: swam 是 swim 的过去式。
children.statement: well 是形容词，用来修饰动词 swam。
children.explanation: well 在这里是副词，用来修饰动词 swam。

children.text: But the other ducklings still laughed at him and told him to go away.
children.cn: 但是其他的小鸭子仍然嘲笑他，并叫他走开。
children.statement: laughed at 表示“嘲笑”，常用被动语态。
children.explanation: laugh at 是固定短语，介词 at 不能省略。

children.text: It made the poor duckling very sad, so he decided to search for a new home.
children.cn: 这让可怜的小鸭子非常难过，所以他决定去寻找一个新家。
children.statement: made 在句中是使役动词，sad 是形容词作宾语补足语。
children.explanation: make sb. + adj. 表示“使某人……”
children.keywords: very sad, search for new home

children.text: He met many other birds, but all of them thought that he was ugly too.
children.cn: 他遇到了许多其他的鸟类，但他们都认为他也长得很丑。
children.notes: thought 是 think 的过去式。
children.statement: thought 是 think 的现在分词形式。
children.explanation: thought 是 think 的过去式或过去分词。

children.text: They did not want him to live with them.
children.cn: 他们不想让他和他们住在一起。
children.notes: want sb. to do sth. 想要某人做某事。
children.explanation: with 是介词，后接人称代词宾格。

children.text: One day, the duckling met a cat and a hen.
children.cn: 有一天，小鸭子遇到了一只猫和一只母鸡。
children.statement: met 是 meet 的过去式。

children.text: 'May I stay with you?' he asked hopefully.
children.cn: '我可以和你们待在一起吗？'他满怀希望地问。
children.statement: hopefully 是副词，修饰动词 asked。
children.explanation: 副词通常用来修饰动词、形容词或其他副词。

children.text: But the two animals said no.
children.cn: 但是这两只动物拒绝了。
children.statement: said 是 say 的过去式。

children.text: They did not like him because he could not purr or lay eggs!
children.cn: 他们不喜欢他，因为他不会发出呼噜声，也不会下蛋！
children.notes: purr 是猫发出的呼噜声；lay eggs 下蛋。
children.statement: because 引导的是时间状语从句。
children.explanation: because 引导的是原因状语从句。

children.text: Some days later, the ugly duckling came to a lake.
children.cn: 几天后，丑小鸭来到了一个湖边。
children.notes: Some days later 几天以后。
children.explanation: lake 的复数形式是 lakes。

children.text: There, he saw three beautiful swans with long necks and white feathers.
children.cn: 在那里，他看到了三只拥有长脖子和白色羽毛的美丽天鹅。
children.notes: swan 意为'天鹅'；feather 意为'羽毛'。
children.statement: saw 是 see 的过去式形式。
children.keywords: beautiful swans, long necks, white feathers

children.text: 'I wish I looked like you!' he cried.
children.cn: '我希望我长得像你们一样！'他哭喊着。
children.statement: looked like 在 wish 引导的从句中表示对现在的虚拟。
children.explanation: wish 后接从句表示愿望时，常用虚拟语气（动词用过去式）。

children.text: 'My friend, you do look like us! Look in the water!' one of the swans said.
children.cn: '我的朋友，你确实长得像我们！往水里看！'其中一只天鹅说。
children.notes: look in the water 往水里看。
children.statement: you do look like us 中的 do 起强调作用。
children.explanation: 助动词 do/does/did 加动词原形可用于加强语气。
children.keywords: look like us, Look in water

children.text: The duckling looked down, and to his surprise, he saw that he was a beautiful white swan too!
children.cn: 小鸭子低头一看，令他惊讶的是，他看到自己也是一只美丽的白色天鹅！
children.notes: to one's surprise 令某人惊讶的是。
children.statement: surprise 在这里是形容词，表示“惊讶的”。
children.explanation: 在这里 surprise 是名词，用于 to one's surprise 短语中。
